screen.itemglint.quick_config.title: Item Outline Quick Config
screen.itemglint.quick_config.general_hi: This screen now focuses only on the held-item outline. Save to write the current layout to the client config.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.hand_hint: Keep at least one hand enabled, otherwise the outline will have nothing to render.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.shape_hint: Width and softness control the edge profile. Larger values make the outline thicker and smoother.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.alpha_hint: Lower alpha thresholds catch more translucent pixels; opacity controls the final strength of the outline itself.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.effects.bl: Bloom settings only affect the halo around the outline. When bloom is disabled, these controls are dimmed.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.effects.mo: Scroll and glow adjust movement and intensity. Reduce them for a cleaner, more static look.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.colors_hin: Pick a target color, then adjust it with the picker, brightness bar, RGB sliders, or a hex value. Secondary color is mainly used by dual-color modes.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.en: Master switch for the held-item outline effect.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.ma: Shows the outline on the item rendered in the main hand.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.of: Shows the outline on the item rendered in the off hand.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.bl: Enables the extra halo pass around the outline. Disable it for a cleaner edge-only look.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.ru: Turns the 0.2-second rule switch delay on or off for non-All modes.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.co: Chooses between a single color, scrolling dual colors, rainbow flow, or automatic sampled palette scrolling.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.ru: Controls whether the effect applies to all held items, only a whitelist, everything except a blacklist, or prioritized custom rules.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.ed: Open the rule editor for whitelist, blacklist, and prioritized custom rules.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.wi: Controls how far the outline expands away from the held item silhouette.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.so: Controls the feathering of the outline edge. Higher values create a softer outer falloff.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.al: Sets the minimum source alpha needed to count as part of the held-item mask. Higher values trim translucent pixels more aggressively.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.op: Scales the final outline visibility after all other effects are applied.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.bl: Changes the internal resolution used for bloom. Lower resolutions are blurrier and cheaper.
Controls how bright and visible the bloom halo becomes.
Controls how far the bloom spreads outward from the outline.
Sets the maximum blur pass count used to build wide bloom radii.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.co: Controls how fast scrolling or rainbow color modes animate.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.de: Boosts outline intensity around strong depth transitions, making the edge read more clearly against the scene.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.gl: Adds extra intensity to the outline rim and bloom halo.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.ac: Selects whether the color tools edit the primary or secondary outline color.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.co: Drag inside the picker to change hue and saturation.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.br: Drag to adjust the brightness/value of the active color.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.he: Type a hex color such as #3AF2FF for the active color.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.re: Adjusts the red channel of the active color.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.gr: Adjusts the green channel of the active color.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.bl: Adjusts the blue channel of the active color.
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.title: Held Item Rule Editor
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.selector_hi: Use minecraft:item_id or #namespace:tag. Example: minecraft:diamond_sword or #forge:tools/swords
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.nbt_hint: NBT path uses dots and [index]. Leave the NBT value empty to match only by path existence.
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.effect_hint: Effect params use key=value pairs separated by commas.
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.effect_para: Color: color_mode, primary, secondary, red, green, blue, secondary_red, secondary_green, secondary_blue
Bloom: bloom, bloom_resolution, bloom_strength, bloom_radius, bloom_max_passes
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.all_mode_hi: All mode renders every held item that passes the normal hand toggles.
Switch to whitelist, blacklist, or custom rules when you want per-item filtering.
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.no_custom_r: Add a rule, then fill in selector/NBT/effect params to override the global effect.
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.selector_hi: Use one selector field: minecraft:item, #namespace:tag, or @modid.
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.no_entries: No entries yet. Add an item id, tag, or mod id above.
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.custom_list: Use + to add a rule. Higher priority rules are matched first.
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.effect_para: Effect keys: width, softness, alpha_threshold, opacity, bloom, bloom_resolution, bloom_strength, bloom_radius, bloom_max_passes
Color/Motion: color_mode, primary, secondary, red, green, blue, secondary_red, secondary_green, secondary_blue, color_scroll_speed, depth_weight, glow_strength
screen.itemglint.selector_browser.title: Browse %s
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.filter_hint: Each filter only checks NBT. Leave the value empty to match by path existence only.
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.tooltip.rul: Name shown in the custom-rule list. Use a short label that helps you identify this rule.
Higher priority rules are matched first.
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.tooltip.nbt: Quickly fill the current filter with a common NBT path and sample value.
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.tooltip.mat: Choose how the NBT value is compared. Greater/less modes only work for numeric NBT.
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.tooltip.nbt: NBT path to inspect, for example display.Name or Enchantments[0].lvl.
Target value for this filter. Leave empty to match only by path existence.
screen.itemglint.rule_editor.tooltip.eff: Quickly apply a preset set of effect parameters.
Override keys such as width, opacity, bloom, primary, color_mode or glow_strength.
Choose which effect parameter to add or overwrite.
Choose a common value preset for the selected effect parameter.
You can type a custom value here, or use the preset value dropdown above.
screen.itemglint.quick_config.tooltip.au: Pixel step used during item texture sampling. Larger values sample fewer pixels.
Maximum number of sampled colors kept in the scrolling palette.
Sort sampled colors by coverage or by hue/saturation for a gradient-like order.
